task_completion.description: Verify that the 'sales_data' CSV file has been imported into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'sales_data' table and its columns (e.g., Region, Sales_Amount, Product_ID) should be visible in the Data or Fields pane on the right side of the Power BI Desktop interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Clustered Bar Chart visualization has been added to the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bar chart visual with horizontal bars (Clustered Bar Chart) should be present on the main report canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the chart uses 'Region' for its categorical axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Y-axis of the horizontal bar chart should display region names (East, North, South, West), or 'Region' should be visible in the Y-axis field well in the Visualizations pane.

task_completion.description: Verify that the chart displays the sum of 'Sales_Amount'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The X-axis should represent numerical values corresponding to sales, and 'Sum of Sales_Amount' (or 'Sales_Amount') should be visible in the X-axis field well in the Visualizations pane. The chart title will typically default to 'Sum of Sales_Amount by Region'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report has been saved with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI Desktop window title bar at the top should read 'sales_report' or 'sales_report.pbix' instead of 'Untitled', or a 'Save As' dialog should be visible saving the file to the Desktop as 'sales_report.pbix'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Power BI Desktop GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Power BI Desktop interface with menus, ribbons, and panes actively being used, rather than just a final static image or terminal window.

integrity.description: Verify the chart reflects the actual dataset provided.
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart must display the specific regions from the synthetic dataset (East, North, South, West) rather than placeholder or manually typed text.

integrity.description: Verify the visualization is a genuine Power BI visual.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual should have standard Power BI interactive elements (like the focus mode icon, more options ellipsis, or highlighting when selected) and should not look like an pasted image from another program.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is synthetic and contains exactly 1000 rows. The 'Region' column contains exactly four generic cardinal directions: East, North, South, and West. A correct clustered bar chart will display these four specific regions on the categorical (Y) axis. The task requires saving the file specifically as 'sales_report.pbix'.
